accurate.why: All provisions accurately described; the bill text does use 'Biden border crisis' and 'illegal aliens' and those are accurately quoted/paraphrased; the 180-day deadline and all report elements are correct.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; all details traceable to the bill text.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies the bill as requiring an AG report on border-related impacts on law enforcement.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly notes the bill creates a reporting requirement only and does not amend existing law.

exceptions_conditions.why: No material exceptions or conditions beyond the report requirement itself.

effective_dates.why: Correctly notes the 180-day deadline for the report.

major_provisions_covered.why: All seven report elements and the congressional findings are thoroughly covered.

objective_tone.why: Presents partisan language in the bill (e.g., 'Biden border crisis') in quotes or with attribution, maintaining neutrality.

concise.why: Very detailed; includes the full evidence lifecycle language from the bill verbatim, which is excessive for a summary.

correct_entities.why: Attorney General, Congress, and section 212(f) of the INA correctly identified.

correct_figures.why: 180-day deadline and 136 officers figure from the findings are correctly cited.

judged_via: claude-sonnet-4-6 via parallel Claude Code subagents (web/knowledge-verified context)
